name: Glenn Zottola
abstract: Glenn Paul Zottola (born April 28, 1947) is an American jazz trumpeter and saxophonist. He is known for his work with Lionel Hampton, Benny Goodman, and Bob Wilber, and has accompanied a broad range of vocalists, including Mel Tormé, Frank Sinatra, Peggy Lee, Ella Fitzgerald, and Joe Williams.

interviews.title: Glenn Zottola interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 7, 2020

concerts.label: 50th Anniversary of Benny Goodman's 1938 Carnegie Hall Concert

works.t: The Girl Friend: The Blue Room
When My Baby Smiles at Me

about_quotes.evidence: I wanted to speak about this project that you’ve been involved with with Glenn Zottola.
